content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a game with two sequences of non-negative integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã and B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äã. He has an integer VVV, which is initially equal to 000. Chef will play for a number of turns he chooses (possibly zero); he stops playing when he gets bored. In each turn of the game, Chef must perform one of the following operations:


Choose an integer XXX from AAA and change VVV to V‚à®XV \lor XV‚à®X, i.e. the bitwise OR of VVV and XXX.
Choose an integer XXX from BBB and change VVV to V‚àßXV \land XV‚àßX, i.e. the bitwise AND of VVV and XXX.
Find the number of possible distinct values which VVV can have after the game ends.
constraints: A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã are pairwise distinct
B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äã are pairwise distinct
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2202^{20}220
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2202^{20}220
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the number of possible values of VVV after the game ends.
